Constraints: PCIe TLPs must conform to PCIe Base Specification. UCIe Flits must conform to UCIe Flit Formats defined in Chapter 2.0/3.0. Error injection rate must be controlled to test BER > 1e-27 scenarios.
Coverage Method: Code Coverage (Line, Toggle, FSM), Functional Coverage (PCIe Transaction Types, Flit Formats, Link States, Error Conditions), Cross Coverage (Transaction Type x Link State, Error Injection x Retry Count)
Description: The verification plan covers the transfer of PCIe protocols over UCIe Links as defined in the PCIe Base Specification, ensuring the Protocol Layer correctly encapsulates PCIe transactions into UCIe Flits and the D2D Adapter handles necessary coordination, CRC/Retry, and Link State Management.
Link to Coverage: Functional Coverage Model for PCIe over UCIe, Link State Machine Coverage, Error Handling Coverage
Pass/Fail Criteria: 1. All PCIe transactions are successfully transferred with correct data integrity. 2. Link Training and Parameter Negotiation complete successfully. 3. CRC errors trigger Retry mechanisms as per Section 3.8. 4. No data loss or corruption during high BER conditions. 5. FSM transitions match UCIe Link State Machine specification.
Randomization Constraints and Rationale: Randomize PCIe TLP types and sizes to ensure comprehensive coverage of protocol handling. Randomize error injection timing to test robustness of Retry logic. Constrain link partner behavior to simulate valid/invalid responses.
Scoreboard and Checker: Scoreboard compares transmitted PCIe TLPs with received PCIe TLPs. Checker validates UCIe Flit structure, CRC values, and Link State Machine transitions against UCIe Spec. Retry logic checker verifies correct behavior per Section 3.8.
Sequence Implementation Notes: Sequences must handle the asynchronous nature of Link Training and Parameter Negotiation. Retry sequences must wait for successful transmission or max retry limit. Monitor D2D Adapter status registers for state changes.
Sequences Coverage: Basic PCIe Read/Write, Atomic Operations, TLPs with different Priorities, Link Training Sequences, Error Injection Sequences (CRC Error, Timeout), Retry Sequences, Power Management Coordination Sequences
Test Case Coverage: PCIe TLP Encapsulation/Decapsulation, Flit Format Validation, Link State Transitions, CRC/Retry Logic, Parameter Exchange, Lane Repair/Reversal (if applicable)
Test Type: Functional Verification, Protocol Conformance, Error Injection, Link Training and Recovery
Testcase Design: Test cases will be designed to exercise the D2D Adapter's role in coordinating the Protocol Layer and Physical Layer, specifically focusing on PCIe protocol transfer, CRC/Retry mechanisms, and Link State Management.
Testcase Inputs: PCIe TLPs (Read, Write, Completion), Error Injection Signals (CRC Error, Timeout), Link Control Signals (Training, Reset)
Testcase Outputs: UCIe Flits on Physical Layer, Received PCIe TLPs at Receiver, Link State Status, Retry Counters, Error Flags
Testcase Steps: 1. Initialize UCIe Link and perform Parameter Negotiation. 2. Establish Link State. 3. Generate PCIe TLPs via DUT. 4. Monitor UCIe Flit transmission. 5. Inject errors (CRC) if testing retry. 6. Verify Retry behavior. 7. Check received PCIe TLPs for integrity. 8. Verify Link State transitions.
Traceability: UCIe Spec Rev 1.1 Sec 1.1.1, Sec 1.1.2, Sec 3.0, Sec 3.8; PCIe Base Specification
UVM Components Involved: uvm_agent (PCIe Agent, UCIe D2D Adapter Agent, PHY Agent), uvm_sequencer (PCIe Sequencer, D2D Sequencer), uvm_driver (PCIe Driver, D2D Driver), uvm_monitor (PCIe Monitor, D2D Monitor), uvm_scoreboard (PCIe-D2D Scoreboard), uvm_env (UCIe Top Level Env)
Verification Goal: Verify that PCIe transactions are correctly encapsulated into UCIe Flits, transmitted over the link, decapsulated at the receiver, and that error handling (CRC/Retry) functions correctly for Raw BER > 1e-27. Ensure Link State Machine and Parameter Negotiation occur correctly between Link Partners.

Constraints: CXL Flits must conform to CXL Specification. RCD/RCH/eRCD/eRCH must be generated to test exclusion.
Coverage Method: Code Coverage, Functional Coverage (CXL Flit types, ARB/MUX states, Retry events), Cross Coverage (CXL Protocol vs. Link State)
Description: The verification plan covers the transfer of CXL protocols over UCIe Links, ensuring that the D2D Adapter correctly handles CXL Flits while explicitly excluding support for RCD, RCH, eRCD, and eRCH types. It also verifies the D2D Adapter's role in ARB/MUX functionality and CRC/Retry schemes for CXL.
Link to Coverage: Functional Coverage Model for CXL Flit Types, ARB/MUX State Machine, Retry Logic
Pass/Fail Criteria: 1. All valid CXL Flits are transferred correctly. 2. RCD/RCH/eRCD/eRCH inputs are ignored or cause an error/status indicating unsupported protocol. 3. CRC errors trigger Retry mechanism as per Section 3.8. 4. ARB/MUX correctly prioritizes CXL traffic against other supported protocols.
Randomization Constraints and Rationale: Randomize CXL transaction types and lengths. Randomize error injection points to cover Retry logic. Constrain unsupported protocols to specific test cases.
Scoreboard and Checker: Compare received CXL Flits with sent Flits. Check for Retry signals on CRC error. Check for error status on unsupported protocol input.
Sequence Implementation Notes: Ensure D2D Adapter is configured to handle CXL. Use UCIe Flit format as defined in Chapter 2.0 and 3.0.
Sequences Coverage: CXL Read/Write/IO sequences, CXL with CRC error injection, CXL with Retry, Mixed protocol arbitration (CXL vs PCIe/Streaming)
Test Case Coverage: CXL Protocol Support, Exclusion of RCD/RCH/eRCD/eRCH, D2D ARB/MUX for CXL, D2D CRC/Retry for CXL
Test Type: Protocol Conformance, Functional Verification, Negative Testing
Testcase Design: Generate CXL transactions, inject errors, verify retry, attempt unsupported protocols
Testcase Inputs: CXL Request/Response Flits, CRC Error patterns, RCD/RCH/eRCD/eRCH Flit patterns
Testcase Outputs: Correct CXL data at receiver, Retry signals generated, Error status for unsupported protocols, Correct ARB/MUX selection
Testcase Steps: 1. Initialize UCIe Link. 2. Configure D2D Adapter for CXL. 3. Send valid CXL Flits. 4. Inject CRC error. 5. Verify Retry. 6. Send RCD/RCH/eRCD/eRCH Flits. 7. Verify rejection/error.
UVM Components Involved: uvm_agent (CXL Agent), uvm_sequencer, uvm_driver, uvm_monitor, uvm_scoreboard, uvm_env
Verification Goal: Verify that CXL protocol Flits are correctly encapsulated, multiplexed, and transmitted over the UCIe link. Verify that RCD/RCH/eRCD/eRCH protocols are rejected or not supported. Verify D2D Adapter handles ARB/MUX and CRC/Retry for CXL traffic.

Constraints: Payload size constraints based on UCIe Flit limits. Error injection rate constrained to simulate BER > 1e-27 for retry testing.
Coverage Method: Code Coverage (Line/Branch), Functional Coverage (Streaming Protocol Modes, Flit Formats, Error Injection, Retry Scenarios), Cross Coverage (Protocol Options x Link State)
Description: The feature enables the transmission of user-defined protocols over UCIe Links via the Streaming Protocol mode, distinct from specific protocol support like PCIe or CXL. It relies on generic modes and associated Flit transfers defined in Chapters 2.0 and 3.0.
Link to Coverage: Functional Coverage Model for Streaming Protocol: Covers Flit types, Payload sizes, Error types, Retry counts.
Link to PDF Coverage: Refer to UCIe Spec Rev 1.1, Section 1.1.1, 1.1.2, Chapter 3.0, Section 3.8.
Link to Register Coverage: Coverage of D2D Adapter configuration registers controlling CRC enable, Retry thresholds, and Protocol Mode selection.
Pass/Fail Criteria: 1. All Streaming Protocol Flit formats are generated and parsed correctly according to Chapter 3.0. 2. CRC calculation and verification pass for all valid data. 3. Retry mechanism triggers and recovers correctly when BER > 1e-27 or error conditions are simulated. 4. Link State Machine transitions correctly during bring-up and parameter negotiation.
Randomization Constraints and Rationale: Randomize payload content to ensure all bit patterns are tested. Randomize error injection timing to cover race conditions in CRC/Retry logic.
Scoreboard and Checker: Scoreboard compares transmitted user-defined payloads with received payloads after de-scrambling and CRC verification. Checker validates Flit headers against Chapter 3.0 formats.
Sequence Implementation Notes: Sequences must handle the asynchronous nature of Link Training and Parameter Negotiation. Ensure sequences wait for 'Link Ready' before transmitting data. Implement specific sequences for CRC error injection.
Sequences Coverage: Coverage of various user-defined payload sizes, burst lengths, idle cycles, and error injection patterns (CRC error, format error) to trigger retry logic.
Target Register and Justification: D2D Adapter Control Register (CRC Enable, Retry Mode), Protocol Negotiation Registers. Justification: These registers configure the D2D Adapter to operate in Streaming Protocol mode and enable the required CRC/Retry functionality.
Test Case Coverage: 1. Basic Streaming Data Transfer. 2. Streaming Data with CRC Check. 3. Streaming Data with CRC Error (Retry). 4. Link Bring-up and Parameter Negotiation for Streaming Mode. 5. High BER Stress Test.
Test Type: Functional Verification, Protocol Conformance, Stress Testing
Testcase Design: Create a UVM testbench with a D2D Adapter agent. Inject random user-defined protocol data. Monitor the FDI (Flit-aware D2D Interface) for correct Flit formation. Introduce errors to test CRC/Retry. Verify Link State transitions.
Testcase Inputs: Random user-defined payload data, Error injection signals (CRC error, bit flip), Link partner simulation responses.
Testcase Outputs: Correct Flit streams on FDI, Correct CRC values, Retry requests/responses, Successful Link State transitions, No data corruption.
Testcase Steps: 1. Reset D2D Adapter. 2. Configure Streaming Protocol Mode. 3. Initiate Link Training and Parameter Negotiation. 4. Transmit streaming data with valid CRC. 5. Inject CRC error. 6. Verify Retry mechanism. 7. Check Link State recovery.
Traceability: UCIe Spec Rev 1.1, Section 1.1.1 (Streaming Protocol), Section 1.1.2 (D2D Adapter CRC/Retry), Chapter 3.0 (Flit Formats), Section 3.8 (Retry Rules).
UVM Components Involved: uvm_agent (D2D Adapter Agent), uvm_sequencer (Streaming Sequencer), uvm_driver (Streaming Driver), uvm_monitor (Streaming Monitor), uvm_scoreboard (Streaming Scoreboard)
Verification Goal: Verify that the UCIe D2D Adapter correctly encapsulates, transmits, and receives user-defined protocol data using the Streaming Protocol modes, ensuring data integrity via CRC/Retry mechanisms where applicable, and correctly negotiates protocol options with the remote link partner.

Constraints: Ensure CXL tests exclude RCD/RCH/eRCD/eRCH modes. Ensure CRC/Retry is triggered only when Raw BER > 1e-27.
Coverage Method: Code Coverage (Line/FSM), Functional Coverage (Protocol Modes, Flit Types, Optimization Flags), Cross Coverage (Protocol x Optimization Mode)
Description: The Protocol Layer supports PCIe, CXL (excluding RCD/RCH/eRCD/eRCH), and Streaming protocols. It utilizes specific optimizations and Flit transfers for each protocol to enable efficient transfer over UCIe Links, with details covered in Chapters 2.0 and 3.0.
Pass/Fail Criteria: All protocol-specific Flit transfers complete without error. CRC/Retry mechanisms function correctly if Raw BER > 1e-27. No unsupported protocols (RCD/RCH/eRCD/eRCH) are accepted or processed. All coverage goals are met.
Randomization Constraints and Rationale: Randomize data payloads within protocol constraints. Randomize optimization mode selection to ensure all modes are tested. Constrain CXL sequences to exclude unsupported controller types.
Scoreboard and Checker: Scoreboard compares transmitted Flits with expected protocol-specific Flit formats. Checker validates CRC/Retry logic and ensures no unsupported protocols are processed.
Sequence Implementation Notes: Sequences must adhere to Flit formats defined in Chapter 3.0. Coordination with D2D Adapter for ARB/MUX and CRC/Retry functionality is required.
Sequences Coverage: Coverage of all supported protocol types (PCIe, CXL, Streaming) and their respective optimization modes. Coverage of edge cases in Flit formatting and transfer sequences.
Test Case Coverage: 100% of defined protocol optimization modes and Flit formats for PCIe, CXL, and Streaming.
Testcase Design: Generate test cases for each supported protocol (PCIe, CXL, Streaming) exercising their specific optimization modes and Flit transfers. Include negative tests for unsupported protocols.
Testcase Inputs: Protocol configuration signals, Data payloads, Control signals for optimization modes, Link partner simulation.
Testcase Outputs: Correctly formatted Flits on the UCIe link, Acknowledgments, Error signals if applicable, Link state updates.
Testcase Steps: 1. Configure DUT for specific protocol (PCIe/CXL/Streaming). 2. Initiate Flit transfer using protocol-specific optimizations. 3. Monitor Flit format and transfer integrity. 4. Verify CRC/Retry if applicable. 5. Check for rejection of unsupported protocols.
Traceability: UCIe Spec Rev 1.1, Sec 1.1.1, Ch 2.0, Ch 3.0
UVM Components Involved: uvm_agent (Protocol Agent), uvm_sequencer (Protocol Sequencer), uvm_driver (Protocol Driver), uvm_monitor (Protocol Monitor), uvm_scoreboard (Protocol Scoreboard)
Verification Goal: Verify that the DUT correctly implements the Protocol Layer optimizations and Flit formats for PCIe, CXL (non-RCD/RCH), and Streaming protocols, ensuring correct data transfer and protocol compliance as defined in Chapters 2.0 and 3.0.
